response:
The error you encounter stems from a version mismatch where your dynamically loaded WPF assembly targets the legacy .NET Framework 4.0 runtime while your host application runs on the modern .NET 7 platform which does not include those specific legacy dependencies by default.

To resolve this without abandoning .NET 7 you must configure your WPF project to target .NET 7 explicitly and ensure it references the modern PresentationFramework assemblies that are part of the .NET 7 SDK rather than the older Framework versions.

cousin projects that rely on cross platform compatibility often fail when they mix runtime targets so you need to verify that your WPF project file specifies the correct TargetFramework property to align with your main application.

Once you update the target framework you should also check that your main application has the necessary runtime configuration to load the WPF window correctly on Windows while the Cocoa path remains untouched for macOS builds.

This approach allows your solution to maintain a single codebase for logic while using the appropriate UI framework for each operating system without forcing a regression to .NET Framework for the entire application.
